rationale_for_baseline_tier: An older adult with a NEW symptomatic conduction delay presenting with recurrent exertional presyncope, central chest heaviness (possible acute coronary syndrome) and exertional dyspnoea. The dangerous mimics — evolving high-grade/complete AV block and acute myocardial ischaemia — cannot be excluded via telehealth (no ECG, no auscultation, no troponin, no rhythm monitoring), and the patient has a high fall risk during presyncope. Near-normal home vitals do not make this safe to defer. The honest telehealth action is an emergency ambulance to the emergency department, matching the source clinician's ambulance dispatch — T5 (call 000).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of consciousness, collapse, or a further near-faint before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 back to upgrade priority and lay the patient flat
escalation_edges.rationale: Syncope or collapse may signal progression to complete heart block or a malignant arrhythmia requiring immediate resuscitation and monitoring.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he passes out or collapses, lay him flat, keep him safe from falling, and call 000 straight back to say it is now an emergency — begin CPR if he stops breathing and you know how.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe or crushing chest pain, chest pain spreading to the arm/jaw, or sweating with the chest heaviness
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 back and report worsening chest pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Escalating chest pain suggests an acute coronary syndrome in evolution and mandates the highest-priority emergency response.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the chest heaviness turns into severe or crushing pain, spreads to your arm or jaw, or you break out in a sweat, call 000 back immediately and tell them your chest pain is getting worse.

escalation_edges.rationale: New or worsening breathlessness at rest signals cardiorespiratory deterioration and warrants immediate emergency response.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become short of breath even while sitting still, call 000 back and let them know your breathing is getting worse.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult (~72) with recurrent presyncope and a high fall risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise strict rest and fall precautions; the spouse should stay with the patient and clear the immediate area, and the patient must not attempt to walk to the door or bathroom unassisted.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is present and able to assist and make the 000 call
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct clear instructions to the spouse: call 000, unlock the door for paramedics, stay with the patient, and report any deterioration immediately.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 now for an ambulance and tell them an older man has repeated dizzy spells with near-fainting and a heavy chest. Stay sitting or lying down and do not walk unassisted — if you feel you are about to faint, lie flat straight away. Have your spouse stay with you, unlock the front door for the paramedics, and keep the phone nearby. This needs an urgent heart tracing (ECG) and blood test at hospital that cannot be done from home.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a routine GP appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide the 12-lead ECG, troponin results and a discharge summary; take these to the GP and cardiology for follow-up of the new conduction abnormality and blood-pressure management.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as benign (routine GP or self-care) — anchoring on 'bundle branch block' and reassuring home vitals.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Unmonitored progression to complete heart block or an unrecognised acute coronary syndrome, risking collapse and sudden death at home.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising a same-day in-person GP or urgent care visit rather than an emergency ambulance, leaving a high-risk cardiac patient without monitoring or timely ECG/troponin.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Not applicable — T5 is the correct action; there is no meaningful over-triage above emergency escalation for this presentation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the presentation is driven by an evolving high-grade AV block, acute coronary ischaemia, or a benign conduction delay cannot be resolved remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate to emergency care so an ECG, cardiac monitoring and troponin can resolve the uncertainty safely; do not attempt a remote diagnosis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Assuming the benign explanation because home vitals are near-normal and deferring to a routine appointment.
